NOTICE OF CONFIDENTIALITY
This document is the property of TRANSWORLD BUSINESS ADVISORS, LLC and represents
general information about a business that TRANSWORLD BUSINESS ADVISORS, LLC represents
for sale on an exclusive basis. This document is not to be copied or distributed, nor its contents in
any way divulged to anyone, as it may contain highly confidential financial and operational
information; and reports that are considered trade secrets by the seller. If you need additional
copies, we will provide them upon qualification of the recipient. Prior to receipt of this Confidential
Memorandum, you should have signed a Confidentiality Agreement, Buyer’s Confidentiality and
Warranty Agreement, or a Non-Disclosure Agreement. DO NOT PROCEED FURTHER UNLESS YOU
HAVE SIGNED ONE OF THESE AGREEMENTS.
While it is normal for you to seek professional advice concerning this opportunity, you should
advise them of their obligation of non-disclosure and to hold this and all other information
concerning this opportunity as confidential, since you will be held liable for the confidentiality
of your advisor(s).
If you receive this as a professional to review and advise a client on the merits of this opportunity,
your professional ethics should prevent the disclosure of this information to any third party.
If you receive this as a potential financing source, you are obligated to keep the information herein
as confidential as possible and are to use it only for making a lending decision. The bearer of this
presentation is responsible for its confidentiality.
This information must be held in absolute confidentiality. Any party reviewing this material
can be held liable for its improper disclosure and could be held liable for damages if such
disclosure proves in any way damaging to the subject company, its owners, employees or
suppliers, or to (your company name), its principals or agents.
DO NOT CONTACT THE OTHER PARTY WITHOUT PROPER AUTHORIZATION. All contacts,
whether by fax, telephone, writing, email or otherwise, require prior approval of TRANSWORLD
BUSINESS ADVISORS, LLC. Failure to adhere to this requirement prior to contacting the other party
WILL jeopardize your ability to pursue this opportunity. If this opportunity involves two brokers,
please work only through TRANSWORLD BUSINESS ADVISORS, LLC.
Thomas I. Jones, P.A.
Transworld Business Advisors
5101 NW 21st Ave., Suite #300
Fort Lauderdale, FL 33309

1

Florida Cuttings, Inc.
2005 SE Cove Rd.
Stuart, FL 34997
BUSINESS OVERVIEW
Established: 2009
Principal: Camile Vitiello
Core Business: Organic Waste pick up and disposal
Geographical Service Area: Palm Beach and St. Lucie Counties, Florida
Real Estate: Beneficial ownership interest via separate entity
available for lease
Employee Count: 8 Drivers
1 Mechanic
Administrative Manager
Operations Manager
Trucks: 9 Grappling Trucks
2023 Revenue/SDE: $2,812,045/$865,755
Florida Cuttings, Inc. (FCI) operates under two subcontractor agreements with Waste Pro of
Florida, Inc. which is the primary contract holder for waste pick up and disposal in Palm
Beach and St. Lucie Counties, Florida. FCI holds the designation of a Florida Small Business
Enterprise (SBE) as a women owned and operated business enterprise. As the holder of the
primary county contracts for waste hauling, Waste Pro of Florida is required to subcontract
part of their overall services with SBE’s and guarantee the SBE minimum revenues set by the
counties. FCI has held these contracts for many years and is confident that the contractual
arrangement will continue to be renewed and extended.
2

Summary of the Contracts
Contract 1: Customer is Waste Pro of Florida, Inc.
• Started October 2019 Expires September 2026
• Commits 1 rear load grapple truck @ $95/hr. minimum 60 hrs/wk.
• 4 – 30 yard grappling trucks at $85/hr. minimum 60 hrs/wk or 50 yard grappling
trucks at $95/wk. minimum of 60 hrs/wk.
• One additional helper as needed for $100/hr.
Contract 2: Customer is Waste Pro of Florida, Inc.
• Started April 2019 Expires March 2024
• Auto renewal for additional five year term to March 2029
• Commits 2 – 30 yard grapple trucks at $85/hr. or 50 yard grapple trucks at $95/hr.
• One additional helper at $100/hr.
Both contracts require FCI to employ their own drivers and carry all the necessary
insurances. Both contracts also specify payment terms to be net 15 days.
Both contracts contain language allowing FCI to terminate with notice while Customer can
only terminate for cause which language also contains a reasonable cure period.
In the geographic areas in which FCI serves trees, shrubs and organics of all kinds grow year round.
Commercial and Residential pick ups of this material is essential to keeping the communities safe
and the streets clear.
3

LOCATION
FCI operates from a 10,000 square foot warehouse facility which Seller controls via a
separate entity. The trucks return daily to this facility and are housed there when not in
use. Regular maintenance and repairs are also carried out in this facility. The facility is
offered for lease which cost at market is included as an expense in the financial analysis
shown in the financial section of this presentation.
